title: Typed follow-up slice drafts through replan
goal: Implement AF-01 from docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/02-typed-drafts.md. Workers and repair workers may emit candidate_followup_slices; the daemon validates each draft using existing slice and area-contract rules and records valid drafts as pending add_followup_slice replan proposals with typed payload and evidence links. Add optional provenance to slice JSON/type/schema. Do not classify, apply, mutate queues, or add autonomy.
acceptance: Worker/repair result schemas accept optional candidate_followup_slices[] carrying full FollowupSliceDraft data without breaking legacy outputs.
Valid follow-up drafts become durable pending add_followup_slice replan proposals with typed payload, draft summary in status/feed, and evidence links to worker output/attempt.
Invalid drafts become warning findings naming the validation error; they do not crash the daemon, fail the attempt, mutate .workflow/slices, or create proposals.
Slice JSON/type/schema accept an optional provenance block while existing hand-written slices remain valid.
No classification, proposal application, queue mutation, generated slice creation, or envelope autonomy behavior is introduced.
must_ask_if: If the implementation would create a second proposal, decision, or apply authority channel outside the existing replan mechanism, stop and ask.
If required runtime behavior cannot be implemented within the declared areas, stop and ask rather than widening scope silently.
If a dependency or prerequisite from docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/00-matrix.md is not satisfied, stop and report the blocker.
If draft validation requires changing the area contract semantics, stop and ask.
